label: yes

So I maintain my initial facing direction throughout. So, if I start facing forward (let's say the positive y-axis), then "right" would be the positive x-direction, "left" is negative x-direction, forward is positive y, backward is negative y. So the direction of movement is fixed based on the initial facing. So let me track each step: 1. Start at (0, 0), facing forward (positive y-axis). 2. Take 3 steps right: Moving in the positive x-direction. New position: (3, 0). 3. Take 1 step backward: Moving in the negative y-direction. New position: (3, -1). 4. Take 6 steps left: Moving in the negative x-direction. New position: (3 - 6 = -3, -1). 5. Take 1 step forward: Moving in the positive y-direction. New position: (-3, -1 + 1 = 0). 6.
